manifest.name: Dialog
manifest.description: The Dialog component provides a highly customizable and accessible modal overlay for presenting focused content or collecting user input. It is built on `@radix-ui/react-dialog` and is designed to temporarily block user interaction with the main application to ensure attention on the dialog's content. Key features include focus trapping, keyboard navigation (e.g., Escape to close), and proper ARIA attribute application for screen reader compatibility. Use cases include confirmation prompts, login forms, detailed views of an item, or any scenario where user focus must be explicitly directed to a specific interaction before proceeding with the rest of the application.

importStatement.primary: import { Dialog, DialogClose, DialogContent, DialogDescription, DialogFooter, DialogHeader, DialogOverlay, DialogPortal, DialogTitle, DialogTrigger } from '@nexus/react'

minimal.title: Default
minimal.code:
( Dialog Title This is a basic dialog with a title and description.

Dialog content goes here. You can add any content you need.

)

common.title: With Description
common.code:
( Edit Profile Make changes to your profile here. Click save when you're done.
)

common.title: No Close Button
common.code:
(
    <Dialog>
      <DialogTrigger asChild>
        <Button variant="outline">Open Dialog</Button>
      </DialogTrigger>
      <DialogContent showCloseButton={false}>
        <DialogHeader>
          <DialogTitle>No Close Button</DialogTitle>
          <DialogDescription>
            This dialog has no close button in the corner. Use the buttons below
            to close.
          </DialogDescription>
        </DialogHeader>
        <DialogFooter>
          <DialogClose asChild>
            <Button variant="outline">Cancel</Button>
          </DialogClose>
          <DialogClose asChild>
            <Button>Confirm</Button>
          </DialogClose>
        </DialogFooter>
      </DialogContent>
    </Dialog>
  )

common.title: Custom Close Button
common.code:
( Custom Close This dialog uses a custom close button in the footer.

You can customize how users close the dialog.

)

common.title: Scrollable Content
common.code:
( Terms of Service Please read the following terms carefully.
{Array.from({ length: 10 }).map((_, i) => (

Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit. Sed do eiusmod tempor incididunt ut labore et dolore magna aliqua. Ut enim ad minim veniam, quis nostrud exercitation ullamco laboris nisi ut aliquip ex ea commodo consequat.

))}
)

guidance.whenToUse: Use when you need to interrupt the user's workflow to present critical information, ask for a decision, or capture focused input. Ideal for confirmations, forms, or detailed views that require full attention.
guidance.whenNotToUse: Avoid using for simple notifications or non-blocking information; use a Toast or Alert for those. Do not use for entire page content; consider a separate page or a Drawer instead if content is complex or scrollable.
guidance.accessibility: Ensure proper focus management, trapping focus within the dialog when open and returning it to the trigger element when closed. Use appropriate ARIA roles (dialog, alertdialog) and attributes (aria-labelledby, aria-describedby) for screen reader users. The dialog should be dismissible via the Escape key and an explicit close button.

subComponents.name: DialogContent
subComponents.description:
DialogContent

The main content container for the dialog. Renders inside a portal with an overlay.

other.name: showCloseButton
other.description: Whether to show the close button in the top-right corner.
